highlighted section  
the dining area is a rectangular space measuring 20 meters in length and 15 meters in width  
the zen garden will be a circular area with a radius of r meters and it will be placed exactly in the center of the dining area  
the total area available for dining tables to be twice the area of the zen garden  
the radius r of the zen garden is calculated as follows  
the area of the zen garden is πr²  
the total area available for dining tables is 2πr²  
the area of the dining area is 20×15=300 square meters  
the area available for dining tables is 300−πr²  
the equation is 300−πr²=2πr²  
solving for r gives r=√(300/(3π))  
the radius r of the zen garden is √(100/π) meters  
highlighted section  
the fountain will be a cylinder with a height of 1.5 meters and a radius that is one-third of the zen garden's radius  
the radius of the fountain is r/3  
the surface area of the cylindrical fountain is 2π(r/3)×1.5  
the total cost to cover the surface area of the cylindrical fountain is 2π(r/3)×1.5×50  
the total cost is 150π(r/3)  
the total cost is 50πr  
the total cost is 50πr dollars  
the total cost is 50πr dollars  
the total cost is 50πr dollars
